content.content:
{"result":"     1	-- Tree Navigation Macros
     2	-- Functions for navigating AST parent-child relationships
     3	-- These leverage the DFS pre-order node_id assignment for O(1) subtree queries
     4	
     5	-- =============================================================================
     6	-- Direct Relationship Helpers
     7	-- =============================================================================
     8	
     9	-- Get immediate children of a node
    10	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_children(my_ast_table, parent_node_id)
    11	-- Note: First argument is a table name (string), second is the parent node_id
    12	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_children(ast_table, parent_node_id) AS TABLE
    13	    SELECT * FROM query_table(ast_table) WHERE parent_id = parent_node_id;
    14	
    15	-- Get arguments of a function call node
    16	-- Returns the actual argument nodes (excludes punctuation like parentheses and commas)
    17	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_call_arguments(my_ast_table, call_node_id)
    18	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_call_arguments(ast_table, call_node_id) AS TABLE
    19	    WITH
    20	        -- Find the argument_list child of the call node
    21	        arg_list AS (
    22	            SELECT node_id as arg_list_id
    23	            FROM query_table(ast_table)
    24	            WHERE parent_id = call_node_id
    25	              AND type IN ('argument_list', 'arguments', 'actual_parameters')
    26	            LIMIT 1
    27	        ),
    28	        -- Get children of argument_list, excluding punctuation
    29	        args AS (
    30	            SELECT
    31	                ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY a.node_id) - 1 AS arg_position,
    32	                a.node_id AS arg_node_id,
    33	                a.name AS arg_name,
    34	                a.type AS arg_type,
    35	                a.peek AS arg_peek,
    36	                a.semantic_type,
    37	                a.start_line,
    38	                a.end_line
    39	            FROM query_table(ast_table) a, arg_list al
    40	            WHERE a.parent_id = al.arg_list_id
    41	              AND a.type NOT IN ('(', ')', ',', 'comment')
    42	        )
    43	    SELECT * FROM args;
    44	
    45	-- Get all definitions (functions, classes, variables, etc.) with unified categories
    46	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_definitions('src/**/*.py')
    47	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_definitions('src/main.py', language := 'python')
    48	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_definitions(source, language := NULL) AS TABLE
    49	    WITH ast AS (
    50	        SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
    51	    )
    52	    SELECT
    53	        a.name,
    54	        CASE
    55	            WHEN is_function_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'function'
    56	            WHEN is_class_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'class'
    57	            WHEN is_variable_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'variable'
    58	            WHEN is_module_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'module'
    59	            WHEN is_type_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'type'
    60	            ELSE 'other'
    61	        END AS definition_type,
    62	        a.language,
    63	        a.file_path,
    64	        a.start_line,
    65	        a.end_line,
    66	        a.node_id,
    67	        a.type,
    68	        a.semantic_type
    69	    FROM ast a
    70	    WHERE is_definition(a.semantic_type)
    71	      AND is_construct(a.flags)
    72	      AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
    73	    ORDER BY a.file_path, a.start_line;
    74	
    75	-- Get all descendants of a node (entire subtree)
    76	-- Uses descendant_count for O(1) range-based lookup (nodes are in DFS pre-order)
    77	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_descendants(my_ast_table, ancestor_node_id)
    78	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_descendants(ast_table, ancestor_node_id) AS TABLE
    79	    WITH ancestor AS (
    80	        SELECT node_id, descendant_count
    81	        FROM query_table(ast_table)
    82	        WHERE node_id = ancestor_node_id
    83	    )
    84	    SELECT a.*
    85	    FROM query_table(ast_table) a, ancestor anc
    86	    WHERE a.node_id > anc.node_id
    87	      AND a.node_id <= anc.node_id + anc.descendant_count;
    88	
    89	-- Get ancestors of a node (path from node to root)
    90	-- Uses recursive CTE following parent_id upward
    91	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_ancestors(my_ast_table, child_node_id)
    92	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_ancestors(ast_table, child_node_id) AS TABLE
    93	    WITH RECURSIVE ancestors AS (
    94	        SELECT * FROM query_table(ast_table) WHERE node_id = child_node_id
    95	        UNION ALL
    96	        SELECT a.*
    97	        FROM query_table(ast_table) a
    98	        JOIN ancestors anc ON a.node_id = anc.parent_id
    99	        WHERE anc.parent_id IS NOT NULL
   100	    )
   101	    SELECT * FROM ancestors;
   102	
   103	-- Get sibling nodes (same parent, excluding self)
   104	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_siblings(my_ast_table, target_node_id)
   105	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_siblings(ast_table, target_node_id) AS TABLE
   106	    WITH target AS (
   107	        SELECT parent_id FROM query_table(ast_table) WHERE node_id = target_node_id
   108	    )
   109	    SELECT a.*
   110	    FROM query_table(ast_table) a, target t
   111	    WHERE a.parent_id = t.parent_id
   112	      AND a.node_id != target_node_id;
   113	
   114	-- =============================================================================
   115	-- Line-Based Navigation
   116	-- =============================================================================
   117	
   118	-- Find all nodes that contain a specific line
   119	-- Returns nodes ordered by specificity (smallest span first)
   120	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_containing_line('src/main.py', 42)
   121	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_containing_line(source, line_num, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   122	    WITH ast AS (
   123	        SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   124	    )
   125	    SELECT a.*
   126	    FROM ast a
   127	    WHERE a.start_line <= line_num AND a.end_line >= line_num
   128	    ORDER BY (a.end_line - a.start_line), a.start_line;
   129	
   130	-- Get all nodes within a line range
   131	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_in_range('src/main.py', 10, 20)
   132	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_in_range(source, range_start, range_end, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   133	    WITH ast AS (
   134	        SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   135	    )
   136	    SELECT a.*
   137	    FROM ast a
   138	    WHERE a.start_line >= range_start AND a.end_line <= range_end;
   139	
   140	-- =============================================================================
   141	-- Scope-Aware Helpers
   142	-- =============================================================================
   143	
   144	-- Get all nodes inside a function, EXCLUDING nested function bodies
   145	-- This is essential for accurate complexity analysis - avoids double-counting
   146	-- nested function internals as part of the outer function's complexity.
   147	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_function_scope(my_ast_table, function_node_id)
   148	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_function_scope(ast_table, func_node_id) AS TABLE
   149	    WITH
   150	        -- Get the function node itself
   151	        func AS (
   152	            SELECT node_id, descendant_count
   153	            FROM query_table(ast_table)
   154	            WHERE node_id = func_node_id
   155	        ),
   156	        -- Get all descendants of this function
   157	        descendants AS (
   158	            SELECT a.*
   159	            FROM query_table(ast_table) a, func f
   160	            WHERE a.node_id > f.node_id
   161	              AND a.node_id <= f.node_id + f.descendant_count
   162	        ),
   163	        -- Find nested function definitions (excluding the function itself)
   164	        nested_funcs AS (
   165	            SELECT node_id, descendant_count
   166	            FROM descendants
   167	            WHERE is_function_definition(semantic_type)
   168	        )
   169	    -- Return descendants that are NOT inside any nested function
   170	    SELECT d.*
   171	    FROM descendants d
   172	    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
   173	        SELECT 1 FROM nested_funcs nf
   174	        WHERE d.node_id > nf.node_id
   175	          AND d.node_id <= nf.node_id + nf.descendant_count
   176	    );
   177	
   178	-- Get direct members of a class (methods, fields, nested classes)
   179	-- Returns the member definition nodes themselves, not their internal contents.
   180	-- Useful for class structure analysis without descending into method bodies.
   181	-- Usage: SELECT name, type FROM ast_class_members(my_ast_table, class_node_id)
   182	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_class_members(ast_table, class_node_id) AS TABLE
   183	    WITH
   184	        -- Get the class node
   185	        class AS (
   186	            SELECT node_id, descendant_count
   187	            FROM query_table(ast_table)
   188	            WHERE node_id = class_node_id
   189	        ),
   190	        -- Get all descendants of the class
   191	        descendants AS (
   192	            SELECT a.*
   193	            FROM query_table(ast_table) a, class c
   194	            WHERE a.node_id > c.node_id
   195	              AND a.node_id <= c.node_id + c.descendant_count
   196	        ),
   197	        -- Find all named definition nodes within the class
   198	        -- Excludes syntactic tokens like 'class'/'def' keywords via flag check
   199	        all_defs AS (
   200	            SELECT node_id, descendant_count, name, type, semantic_type,
   201	                   start_line, end_line, depth, parent_id, peek, file_path, language
   202	            FROM descendants
   203	            WHERE is_definition(semantic_type)
   204	              AND is_construct(flags)
   205	              AND name IS NOT NULL AND name != ''
   206	        )
   207	    -- Return definitions that are NOT nested inside other definitions
   208	    -- (i.e., direct members of the class, not locals inside methods)
   209	    SELECT d.*
   210	    FROM all_defs d
   211	    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
   212	        SELECT 1 FROM all_defs other
   213	        WHERE other.node_id != d.node_id
   214	          AND d.node_id > other.node_id
   215	          AND d.node_id <= other.node_id + other.descendant_count
   216	    );
   217	
   218	-- =============================================================================
   219	-- Analysis Macros
   220	-- =============================================================================
   221	
   222	-- Compute metrics for all functions in the AST
   223	-- Returns one row per function with complexity metrics.
   224	-- Uses function scope (excludes nested function internals) for accurate counts.
   225	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_function_metrics('src/**/*.py') WHERE cyclomatic > 10
   226	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_function_metrics(source, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   227	    WITH
   228	        ast AS (
   229	            SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   230	        ),
   231	        -- All function definitions
   232	        functions AS (
   233	            SELECT
   234	                a.node_id AS func_id,
   235	                a.name,
   236	                a.file_path,
   237	                a.language,
   238	                a.start_line,
   239	                a.end_line,
   240	                a.depth AS func_depth,
   241	                a.descendant_count
   242	            FROM ast a
   243	            WHERE is_function_definition(a.semantic_type)
   244	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   245	        ),
   246	        -- For each function, identify nested functions within it
   247	        nested_funcs AS (
   248	            SELECT
   249	                nf.node_id AS nested_id,
   250	                nf.descendant_count AS nested_count,
   251	                f.func_id AS parent_func_id
   252	            FROM functions f
   253	            JOIN ast nf
   254	              ON nf.node_id > f.func_id
   255	             AND nf.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   256	             AND is_function_definition(nf.semantic_type)
   257	        ),
   258	        -- Compute metrics for each function
   259	        -- Counts only nodes in function scope (excludes nested function internals)
   260	        function_metrics AS (
   261	            SELECT
   262	                f.func_id,
   263	                f.file_path,
   264	                f.name,
   265	                f.language,
   266	                f.start_line,
   267	                f.end_line,
   268	                f.func_depth,
   269	                -- Count return statements (not the 'return' keyword, just the statement)
   270	                COUNT(CASE
   271	                    WHEN n.type = 'return_statement'
   272	                    THEN 1
   273	                END) AS return_count,
   274	                -- Count conditionals: statements/clauses only, not keywords
   275	                -- (if_statement, elif_clause, switch_statement, match_arm, etc.)
   276	                COUNT(CASE
   277	                    WHEN is_conditional(n.semantic_type)
   278	                     AND (n.type LIKE '%_statement' OR n.type LIKE '%_clause'
   279	                          OR n.type LIKE '%_expression' OR n.type LIKE '%_arm'
   280	                          OR n.type LIKE '%_case' OR n.type LIKE '%_branch')
   281	                    THEN 1
   282	                END) AS conditionals,
   283	                -- Count loops: statements only, not keywords
   284	                -- (for_statement, while_statement, etc.)
   285	                COUNT(CASE
   286	                    WHEN is_loop(n.semantic_type)
   287	                     AND (n.type LIKE '%_statement' OR n.type LIKE '%_expression'
   288	                          OR n.type LIKE '%_loop')
   289	                    THEN 1
   290	                END) AS loops,
   291	                -- Max depth of any node in scope
   292	                MAX(n.depth) AS max_node_depth
   293	            FROM functions f
   294	            LEFT JOIN ast n
   295	              ON n.node_id > f.func_id
   296	             AND n.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   297	            WHERE
   298	                -- Exclude nodes inside nested functions
   299	                NOT EXISTS (
   300	                    SELECT 1 FROM nested_funcs nf
   301	                    WHERE nf.parent_func_id = f.func_id
   302	                      AND n.node_id > nf.nested_id
   303	                      AND n.node_id <= nf.nested_id + nf.nested_count
   304	                )
   305	            GROUP BY f.func_id, f.file_path, f.name, f.language,
   306	                     f.start_line, f.end_line, f.func_depth
   307	        )
   308	    SELECT
   309	        fm.file_path,
   310	        fm.name,
   311	        fm.language,
   312	        fm.start_line,
   313	        fm.end_line,
   314	        fm.end_line - fm.start_line + 1 AS lines,
   315	        fm.return_count,
   316	        fm.conditionals,
   317	        fm.loops,
   318	        fm.conditionals + fm.loops + 1 AS cyclomatic,
   319	        COALESCE(fm.max_node_depth::INTEGER - fm.func_depth::INTEGER, 0) AS max_depth
   320	    FROM function_metrics fm
   321	    ORDER BY fm.file_path, fm.start_line;
   322	
   323	-- Find functions that contain a specific node type
   324	-- Returns functions with at least one matching node in their scope.
   325	-- Uses function scope (excludes nested function internals).
   326	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_functions_containing('src/**/*.py', 'try_statement')
   327	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_functions_containing('src/**/*.py', 'call') WHERE match_name = 'eval'
   328	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_functions_containing(source, target_type, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   329	    WITH
   330	        ast AS (
   331	            SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   332	        ),
   333	        -- All function definitions
   334	        functions AS (
   335	            SELECT
   336	                a.node_id AS func_id,
   337	                a.name AS func_name,
   338	                a.file_path,
   339	                a.language,
   340	                a.start_line,
   341	                a.end_line,
   342	                a.descendant_count
   343	            FROM ast a
   344	            WHERE is_function_definition(a.semantic_type)
   345	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   346	        ),
   347	        -- For each function, identify nested functions within it
   348	        nested_funcs AS (
   349	            SELECT
   350	                nf.node_id AS nested_id,
   351	                nf.descendant_count AS nested_count,
   352	                f.func_id AS parent_func_id
   353	            FROM functions f
   354	            JOIN ast nf
   355	              ON nf.node_id > f.func_id
   356	             AND nf.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   357	             AND is_function_definition(nf.semantic_type)
   358	        ),
   359	        -- Find matching nodes in each function's scope
   360	        matches AS (
   361	            SELECT
   362	                f.func_id,
   363	                f.func_name,
   364	                f.file_path,
   365	                f.language,
   366	                f.start_line,
   367	                f.end_line,
   368	                n.node_id AS match_node_id,
   369	                n.name AS match_name,
   370	                n.type AS match_type,
   371	                n.start_line AS match_line,
   372	                n.peek AS match_peek
   373	            FROM functions f
   374	            JOIN ast n
   375	              ON n.node_id > f.func_id
   376	             AND n.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   377	             AND n.type = target_type
   378	            WHERE
   379	                -- Exclude nodes inside nested functions
   380	                NOT EXISTS (
   381	                    SELECT 1 FROM nested_funcs nf
   382	                    WHERE nf.parent_func_id = f.func_id
   383	                      AND n.node_id > nf.nested_id
   384	                      AND n.node_id <= nf.nested_id + nf.nested_count
   385	                )
   386	        )
   387	    SELECT
   388	        m.file_path,
   389	        m.func_name,
   390	        m.language,
   391	        m.start_line AS func_start_line,
   392	        m.end_line AS func_end_line,
   393	        m.match_name,
   394	        m.match_line,
   395	        m.match_peek
   396	    FROM matches m
   397	    ORDER BY m.file_path, func_start_line, m.match_line;
   398	
   399	-- Analyze nesting depth per function
   400	-- Returns depth statistics for identifying deeply nested code.
   401	-- Uses function scope (excludes nested function internals).
   402	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_nesting_analysis('src/**/*.py') WHERE max_depth > 5
   403	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_nesting_analysis(source, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   404	    WITH
   405	        ast AS (
   406	            SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   407	        ),
   408	        -- All function definitions
   409	        functions AS (
   410	            SELECT
   411	                a.node_id AS func_id,
   412	                a.name,
   413	                a.file_path,
   414	                a.language,
   415	                a.start_line,
   416	                a.end_line,
   417	                a.depth AS func_depth,
   418	                a.descendant_count
   419	            FROM ast a
   420	            WHERE is_function_definition(a.semantic_type)
   421	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   422	        ),
   423	        -- For each function, identify nested functions within it
   424	        nested_funcs AS (
   425	            SELECT
   426	                nf.node_id AS nested_id,
   427	                nf.descendant_count AS nested_count,
   428	                f.func_id AS parent_func_id
   429	            FROM functions f
   430	            JOIN ast nf
   431	              ON nf.node_id > f.func_id
   432	             AND nf.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   433	             AND is_function_definition(nf.semantic_type)
   434	        ),
   435	        -- Compute depth statistics for each function
   436	        depth_stats AS (
   437	            SELECT
   438	                f.func_id,
   439	                f.file_path,
   440	                f.name,
   441	                f.language,
   442	                f.start_line,
   443	                f.end_line,
   444	                f.func_depth,
   445	                -- Max relative depth (cast to INTEGER to avoid UINT32 overflow)
   446	                MAX(n.depth::INTEGER - f.func_depth::INTEGER) AS max_depth,
   447	                -- Average relative depth
   448	                ROUND(AVG(n.depth::INTEGER - f.func_depth::INTEGER), 2) AS avg_depth,
   449	                -- Count of deeply nested nodes (relative depth > 5)
   450	                COUNT(CASE WHEN n.depth::INTEGER - f.func_depth::INTEGER > 5 THEN 1 END) AS deep_nodes,
   451	                -- Total nodes in scope
   452	                COUNT(*) AS total_nodes
   453	            FROM functions f
   454	            LEFT JOIN ast n
   455	              ON n.node_id > f.func_id
   456	             AND n.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   457	            WHERE
   458	                -- Exclude nodes inside nested functions
   459	                NOT EXISTS (
   460	                    SELECT 1 FROM nested_funcs nf
   461	                    WHERE nf.parent_func_id = f.func_id
   462	                      AND n.node_id > nf.nested_id
   463	                      AND n.node_id <= nf.nested_id + nf.nested_count
   464	                )
   465	            GROUP BY f.func_id, f.file_path, f.name, f.language,
   466	                     f.start_line, f.end_line, f.func_depth
   467	        )
   468	    SELECT
   469	        ds.file_path,
   470	        ds.name,
   471	        ds.language,
   472	        ds.start_line,
   473	        ds.end_line,
   474	        ds.max_depth,
   475	        ds.avg_depth,
   476	        ds.deep_nodes,
   477	        ds.total_nodes
   478	    FROM depth_stats ds
   479	    ORDER BY ds.max_depth DESC, ds.file_path, ds.start_line;
   480	
   481	-- Automated security concern detection
   482	-- Scans for common security anti-patterns across languages.
   483	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_security_audit('src/**/*.py') WHERE risk_level = 'high'
   484	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_security_audit(source, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   485	    WITH
   486	        ast AS (
   487	            SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   488	        ),
   489	        -- Define security patterns to detect
   490	        security_patterns AS (
   491	            SELECT * FROM (VALUES
   492	                -- Code Injection (High Risk)
   493	                ('eval', 'Code Injection', 'high', 'Dynamic code evaluation'),
   494	                ('exec', 'Code Injection', 'high', 'Dynamic code execution'),
   495	                ('compile', 'Code Injection', 'high', 'Dynamic code compilation'),
   496	                ('Function', 'Code Injection', 'high', 'Dynamic function creation'),
   497	                ('setInterval', 'Code Injection', 'medium', 'Potential code injection via string'),
   498	                ('setTimeout', 'Code Injection', 'medium', 'Potential code injection via string'),
   499	                -- Command Injection (High Risk)
   500	                ('system', 'Command Injection', 'high', 'Shell command execution'),
   501	                ('popen', 'Command Injection', 'high', 'Shell command execution'),
   502	                ('spawn', 'Command Injection', 'high', 'Process spawning'),
   503	                ('execSync', 'Command Injection', 'high', 'Synchronous command execution'),
   504	                ('execFile', 'Command Injection', 'high', 'File execution'),
   505	                ('ShellExecute', 'Command Injection', 'high', 'Windows shell execution'),
   506	                -- Deserialization (High Risk)
   507	                ('pickle.load', 'Deserialization', 'high', 'Unsafe pickle deserialization'),
   508	                ('pickle.loads', 'Deserialization', 'high', 'Unsafe pickle deserialization'),
   509	                ('yaml.load', 'Deserialization', 'high', 'Unsafe YAML loading'),
   510	                ('unserialize', 'Deserialization', 'high', 'PHP unsafe deserialization'),
   511	                ('Marshal.load', 'Deserialization', 'high', 'Ruby unsafe deserialization'),
   512	                ('readObject', 'Deserialization', 'medium', 'Java deserialization'),
   513	                -- SQL Injection indicators (Medium Risk)
   514	                ('execute', 'SQL Injection', 'medium', 'SQL execution - verify parameterized'),
   515	                ('executemany', 'SQL Injection', 'medium', 'SQL execution - verify parameterized'),
   516	                ('raw', 'SQL Injection', 'medium', 'Raw SQL query'),
   517	                ('rawQuery', 'SQL Injection', 'medium', 'Raw SQL query'),
   518	                -- File Operations (Medium Risk)
   519	                ('readFile', 'Path Traversal', 'medium', 'File read - verify path sanitization'),
   520	                ('writeFile', 'Path Traversal', 'medium', 'File write - verify path sanitization'),
   521	                ('unlink', 'Path Traversal', 'medium', 'File deletion - verify path sanitization'),
   522	                ('rmdir', 'Path Traversal', 'medium', 'Directory deletion - verify path'),
   523	                -- Crypto Concerns (Medium Risk)
   524	                ('md5', 'Weak Crypto', 'medium', 'MD5 is cryptographically weak'),
   525	                ('sha1', 'Weak Crypto', 'medium', 'SHA1 is cryptographically weak'),
   526	                ('DES', 'Weak Crypto', 'medium', 'DES encryption is weak'),
   527	                ('RC4', 'Weak Crypto', 'medium', 'RC4 encryption is weak'),
   528	                -- Debug/Development (Low Risk)
   529	                ('console.log', 'Debug Code', 'low', 'Debug logging in code'),
   530	                ('print', 'Debug Code', 'low', 'Print statement - may leak info'),
   531	                ('debugger', 'Debug Code', 'low', 'Debugger statement'),
   532	                ('assert', 'Debug Code', 'low', 'Assert statement')
   533	            ) AS t(pattern_name, risk_category, risk_level, description)
   534	        ),
   535	        -- Find all function calls in the AST
   536	        calls AS (
   537	            SELECT
   538	                a.node_id,
   539	                a.file_path,
   540	                a.language,
   541	                a.name,
   542	                a.start_line,
   543	                a.peek
   544	            FROM ast a
   545	            WHERE is_function_call(a.semantic_type)
   546	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   547	        ),
   548	        -- Find the containing function for each call
   549	        containing_funcs AS (
   550	            SELECT
   551	                c.node_id AS call_node_id,
   552	                f.name AS function_name
   553	            FROM calls c
   554	            LEFT JOIN ast f
   555	              ON f.node_id < c.node_id
   556	             AND c.node_id <= f.node_id + f.descendant_count
   557	             AND is_function_definition(f.semantic_type)
   558	             AND f.name IS NOT NULL AND f.name != ''
   559	            QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY c.node_id ORDER BY f.node_id DESC) = 1
   560	        ),
   561	        -- Match calls against security patterns
   562	        -- Checks call name and peek (context) for qualified names like pickle.load
   563	        findings AS (
   564	            SELECT
   565	                c.file_path,
   566	                c.language,
   567	                c.start_line,
   568	                COALESCE(cf.function_name, '<module>') AS function_name,
   569	                p.risk_category,
   570	                p.risk_level,
   571	                p.description AS finding,
   572	                c.name AS matched_pattern,
   573	                c.peek AS context
   574	            FROM calls c
   575	            JOIN security_patterns p
   576	              ON c.name = p.pattern_name
   577	              OR c.name LIKE '%.' || p.pattern_name
   578	              OR c.name LIKE p.pattern_name || '.%'
   579	              OR c.peek LIKE '%' || p.pattern_name || '%'
   580	            LEFT JOIN containing_funcs cf ON cf.call_node_id = c.node_id
   581	        )
   582	    SELECT
   583	        fi.file_path,
   584	        fi.language,
   585	        fi.start_line,
   586	        fi.function_name,
   587	        fi.risk_category,
   588	        fi.risk_level,
   589	        fi.finding,
   590	        fi.matched_pattern,
   591	        fi.context
   592	    FROM findings fi
   593	    ORDER BY
   594	        CASE fi.risk_level WHEN 'high' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END,
   595	        fi.file_path, fi.start_line;
   596	
   597	-- Find potentially dead (unused) code
   598	-- Identifies functions and classes that are never referenced in the codebase.
   599	-- Note: This is heuristic - cannot detect dynamic usage or cross-file references
   600	-- when analyzing a single file. Best used on entire codebase.
   601	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_dead_code('src/**/*.py')
   602	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_dead_code(source, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   603	    WITH
   604	        ast AS (
   605	            SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   606	        ),
   607	        -- All function definitions
   608	        function_defs AS (
   609	            SELECT
   610	                a.node_id,
   611	                a.name,
   612	                a.file_path,
   613	                a.language,
   614	                a.start_line,
   615	                a.end_line,
   616	                a.type,
   617	                'function' AS definition_type
   618	            FROM ast a
   619	            WHERE is_function_definition(a.semantic_type)
   620	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   621	              -- Exclude special methods (constructors, dunder methods, etc.)
   622	              AND a.name NOT LIKE '\_\_%' ESCAPE '\'
   623	              AND a.name NOT IN ('main', 'setup', 'teardown', 'init', 'constructor')
   624	        ),
   625	        -- All class definitions
   626	        class_defs AS (
   627	            SELECT
   628	                a.node_id,
   629	                a.name,
   630	                a.file_path,
   631	                a.language,
   632	                a.start_line,
   633	                a.end_line,
   634	                a.type,
   635	                'class' AS definition_type
   636	            FROM ast a
   637	            WHERE is_class_definition(a.semantic_type)
   638	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   639	        ),
   640	        -- All definitions combined
   641	        all_defs AS (
   642	            SELECT * FROM function_defs
   643	            UNION ALL
   644	            SELECT * FROM class_defs
   645	        ),
   646	        -- All function calls (references to functions)
   647	        function_calls AS (
   648	            SELECT DISTINCT a.name
   649	            FROM ast a
   650	            WHERE is_function_call(a.semantic_type)
   651	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   652	        ),
   653	        -- All identifier references (for classes and other references)
   654	        -- Exclude identifiers that are the name of a definition (parent is definition type)
   655	        identifier_refs AS (
   656	            SELECT DISTINCT a.name
   657	            FROM ast a
   658	            LEFT JOIN ast parent ON parent.node_id = a.parent_id
   659	            WHERE is_identifier(a.semantic_type)
   660	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   661	              -- Exclude identifiers that are names within definitions
   662	              AND (parent.semantic_type IS NULL
   663	                   OR (NOT is_function_definition(parent.semantic_type)
   664	                       AND NOT is_class_definition(parent.semantic_type)))
   665	        ),
   666	        -- All references combined
   667	        all_refs AS (
   668	            SELECT name FROM function_calls
   669	            UNION
   670	            SELECT name FROM identifier_refs
   671	        ),
   672	        -- Find definitions that are never referenced
   673	        dead_code AS (
   674	            SELECT
   675	                d.file_path,
   676	                d.name,
   677	                d.language,
   678	                d.start_line,
   679	                d.end_line,
   680	                d.type,
   681	                d.definition_type,
   682	                'Never referenced in codebase' AS reason
   683	            FROM all_defs d
   684	            WHERE NOT EXISTS (
   685	                SELECT 1 FROM all_refs r
   686	                WHERE r.name = d.name
   687	            )
   688	        )
   689	    SELECT
   690	        dc.file_path,
   691	        dc.name,
   692	        dc.language,
   693	        dc.start_line,
   694	        dc.end_line,
   695	        dc.type,
   696	        dc.definition_type,
   697	        dc.reason
   698	    FROM dead_code dc
   699	    ORDER BY dc.file_path, dc.start_line;
   700	
"}

tool_use_result.content:
{"result":"     1	-- Tree Navigation Macros
     2	-- Functions for navigating AST parent-child relationships
     3	-- These leverage the DFS pre-order node_id assignment for O(1) subtree queries
     4	
     5	-- =============================================================================
     6	-- Direct Relationship Helpers
     7	-- =============================================================================
     8	
     9	-- Get immediate children of a node
    10	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_children(my_ast_table, parent_node_id)
    11	-- Note: First argument is a table name (string), second is the parent node_id
    12	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_children(ast_table, parent_node_id) AS TABLE
    13	    SELECT * FROM query_table(ast_table) WHERE parent_id = parent_node_id;
    14	
    15	-- Get arguments of a function call node
    16	-- Returns the actual argument nodes (excludes punctuation like parentheses and commas)
    17	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_call_arguments(my_ast_table, call_node_id)
    18	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_call_arguments(ast_table, call_node_id) AS TABLE
    19	    WITH
    20	        -- Find the argument_list child of the call node
    21	        arg_list AS (
    22	            SELECT node_id as arg_list_id
    23	            FROM query_table(ast_table)
    24	            WHERE parent_id = call_node_id
    25	              AND type IN ('argument_list', 'arguments', 'actual_parameters')
    26	            LIMIT 1
    27	        ),
    28	        -- Get children of argument_list, excluding punctuation
    29	        args AS (
    30	            SELECT
    31	                ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY a.node_id) - 1 AS arg_position,
    32	                a.node_id AS arg_node_id,
    33	                a.name AS arg_name,
    34	                a.type AS arg_type,
    35	                a.peek AS arg_peek,
    36	                a.semantic_type,
    37	                a.start_line,
    38	                a.end_line
    39	            FROM query_table(ast_table) a, arg_list al
    40	            WHERE a.parent_id = al.arg_list_id
    41	              AND a.type NOT IN ('(', ')', ',', 'comment')
    42	        )
    43	    SELECT * FROM args;
    44	
    45	-- Get all definitions (functions, classes, variables, etc.) with unified categories
    46	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_definitions('src/**/*.py')
    47	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_definitions('src/main.py', language := 'python')
    48	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_definitions(source, language := NULL) AS TABLE
    49	    WITH ast AS (
    50	        SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
    51	    )
    52	    SELECT
    53	        a.name,
    54	        CASE
    55	            WHEN is_function_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'function'
    56	            WHEN is_class_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'class'
    57	            WHEN is_variable_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'variable'
    58	            WHEN is_module_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'module'
    59	            WHEN is_type_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'type'
    60	            ELSE 'other'
    61	        END AS definition_type,
    62	        a.language,
    63	        a.file_path,
    64	        a.start_line,
    65	        a.end_line,
    66	        a.node_id,
    67	        a.type,
    68	        a.semantic_type
    69	    FROM ast a
    70	    WHERE is_definition(a.semantic_type)
    71	      AND is_construct(a.flags)
    72	      AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
    73	    ORDER BY a.file_path, a.start_line;
    74	
    75	-- Get all descendants of a node (entire subtree)
    76	-- Uses descendant_count for O(1) range-based lookup (nodes are in DFS pre-order)
    77	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_descendants(my_ast_table, ancestor_node_id)
    78	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_descendants(ast_table, ancestor_node_id) AS TABLE
    79	    WITH ancestor AS (
    80	        SELECT node_id, descendant_count
    81	        FROM query_table(ast_table)
    82	        WHERE node_id = ancestor_node_id
    83	    )
    84	    SELECT a.*
    85	    FROM query_table(ast_table) a, ancestor anc
    86	    WHERE a.node_id > anc.node_id
    87	      AND a.node_id <= anc.node_id + anc.descendant_count;
    88	
    89	-- Get ancestors of a node (path from node to root)
    90	-- Uses recursive CTE following parent_id upward
    91	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_ancestors(my_ast_table, child_node_id)
    92	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_ancestors(ast_table, child_node_id) AS TABLE
    93	    WITH RECURSIVE ancestors AS (
    94	        SELECT * FROM query_table(ast_table) WHERE node_id = child_node_id
    95	        UNION ALL
    96	        SELECT a.*
    97	        FROM query_table(ast_table) a
    98	        JOIN ancestors anc ON a.node_id = anc.parent_id
    99	        WHERE anc.parent_id IS NOT NULL
   100	    )
   101	    SELECT * FROM ancestors;
   102	
   103	-- Get sibling nodes (same parent, excluding self)
   104	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_siblings(my_ast_table, target_node_id)
   105	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_siblings(ast_table, target_node_id) AS TABLE
   106	    WITH target AS (
   107	        SELECT parent_id FROM query_table(ast_table) WHERE node_id = target_node_id
   108	    )
   109	    SELECT a.*
   110	    FROM query_table(ast_table) a, target t
   111	    WHERE a.parent_id = t.parent_id
   112	      AND a.node_id != target_node_id;
   113	
   114	-- =============================================================================
   115	-- Line-Based Navigation
   116	-- =============================================================================
   117	
   118	-- Find all nodes that contain a specific line
   119	-- Returns nodes ordered by specificity (smallest span first)
   120	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_containing_line('src/main.py', 42)
   121	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_containing_line(source, line_num, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   122	    WITH ast AS (
   123	        SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   124	    )
   125	    SELECT a.*
   126	    FROM ast a
   127	    WHERE a.start_line <= line_num AND a.end_line >= line_num
   128	    ORDER BY (a.end_line - a.start_line), a.start_line;
   129	
   130	-- Get all nodes within a line range
   131	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_in_range('src/main.py', 10, 20)
   132	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_in_range(source, range_start, range_end, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   133	    WITH ast AS (
   134	        SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   135	    )
   136	    SELECT a.*
   137	    FROM ast a
   138	    WHERE a.start_line >= range_start AND a.end_line <= range_end;
   139	
   140	-- =============================================================================
   141	-- Scope-Aware Helpers
   142	-- =============================================================================
   143	
   144	-- Get all nodes inside a function, EXCLUDING nested function bodies
   145	-- This is essential for accurate complexity analysis - avoids double-counting
   146	-- nested function internals as part of the outer function's complexity.
   147	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_function_scope(my_ast_table, function_node_id)
   148	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_function_scope(ast_table, func_node_id) AS TABLE
   149	    WITH
   150	        -- Get the function node itself
   151	        func AS (
   152	            SELECT node_id, descendant_count
   153	            FROM query_table(ast_table)
   154	            WHERE node_id = func_node_id
   155	        ),
   156	        -- Get all descendants of this function
   157	        descendants AS (
   158	            SELECT a.*
   159	            FROM query_table(ast_table) a, func f
   160	            WHERE a.node_id > f.node_id
   161	              AND a.node_id <= f.node_id + f.descendant_count
   162	        ),
   163	        -- Find nested function definitions (excluding the function itself)
   164	        nested_funcs AS (
   165	            SELECT node_id, descendant_count
   166	            FROM descendants
   167	            WHERE is_function_definition(semantic_type)
   168	        )
   169	    -- Return descendants that are NOT inside any nested function
   170	    SELECT d.*
   171	    FROM descendants d
   172	    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
   173	        SELECT 1 FROM nested_funcs nf
   174	        WHERE d.node_id > nf.node_id
   175	          AND d.node_id <= nf.node_id + nf.descendant_count
   176	    );
   177	
   178	-- Get direct members of a class (methods, fields, nested classes)
   179	-- Returns the member definition nodes themselves, not their internal contents.
   180	-- Useful for class structure analysis without descending into method bodies.
   181	-- Usage: SELECT name, type FROM ast_class_members(my_ast_table, class_node_id)
   182	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_class_members(ast_table, class_node_id) AS TABLE
   183	    WITH
   184	        -- Get the class node
   185	        class AS (
   186	            SELECT node_id, descendant_count
   187	            FROM query_table(ast_table)
   188	            WHERE node_id = class_node_id
   189	        ),
   190	        -- Get all descendants of the class
   191	        descendants AS (
   192	            SELECT a.*
   193	            FROM query_table(ast_table) a, class c
   194	            WHERE a.node_id > c.node_id
   195	              AND a.node_id <= c.node_id + c.descendant_count
   196	        ),
   197	        -- Find all named definition nodes within the class
   198	        -- Excludes syntactic tokens like 'class'/'def' keywords via flag check
   199	        all_defs AS (
   200	            SELECT node_id, descendant_count, name, type, semantic_type,
   201	                   start_line, end_line, depth, parent_id, peek, file_path, language
   202	            FROM descendants
   203	            WHERE is_definition(semantic_type)
   204	              AND is_construct(flags)
   205	              AND name IS NOT NULL AND name != ''
   206	        )
   207	    -- Return definitions that are NOT nested inside other definitions
   208	    -- (i.e., direct members of the class, not locals inside methods)
   209	    SELECT d.*
   210	    FROM all_defs d
   211	    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
   212	        SELECT 1 FROM all_defs other
   213	        WHERE other.node_id != d.node_id
   214	          AND d.node_id > other.node_id
   215	          AND d.node_id <= other.node_id + other.descendant_count
   216	    );
   217	
   218	-- =============================================================================
   219	-- Analysis Macros
   220	-- =============================================================================
   221	
   222	-- Compute metrics for all functions in the AST
   223	-- Returns one row per function with complexity metrics.
   224	-- Uses function scope (excludes nested function internals) for accurate counts.
   225	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_function_metrics('src/**/*.py') WHERE cyclomatic > 10
   226	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_function_metrics(source, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   227	    WITH
   228	        ast AS (
   229	            SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   230	        ),
   231	        -- All function definitions
   232	        functions AS (
   233	            SELECT
   234	                a.node_id AS func_id,
   235	                a.name,
   236	                a.file_path,
   237	                a.language,
   238	                a.start_line,
   239	                a.end_line,
   240	                a.depth AS func_depth,
   241	                a.descendant_count
   242	            FROM ast a
   243	            WHERE is_function_definition(a.semantic_type)
   244	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   245	        ),
   246	        -- For each function, identify nested functions within it
   247	        nested_funcs AS (
   248	            SELECT
   249	                nf.node_id AS nested_id,
   250	                nf.descendant_count AS nested_count,
   251	                f.func_id AS parent_func_id
   252	            FROM functions f
   253	            JOIN ast nf
   254	              ON nf.node_id > f.func_id
   255	             AND nf.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   256	             AND is_function_definition(nf.semantic_type)
   257	        ),
   258	        -- Compute metrics for each function
   259	        -- Counts only nodes in function scope (excludes nested function internals)
   260	        function_metrics AS (
   261	            SELECT
   262	                f.func_id,
   263	                f.file_path,
   264	                f.name,
   265	                f.language,
   266	                f.start_line,
   267	                f.end_line,
   268	                f.func_depth,
   269	                -- Count return statements (not the 'return' keyword, just the statement)
   270	                COUNT(CASE
   271	                    WHEN n.type = 'return_statement'
   272	                    THEN 1
   273	                END) AS return_count,
   274	                -- Count conditionals: statements/clauses only, not keywords
   275	                -- (if_statement, elif_clause, switch_statement, match_arm, etc.)
   276	                COUNT(CASE
   277	                    WHEN is_conditional(n.semantic_type)
   278	                     AND (n.type LIKE '%_statement' OR n.type LIKE '%_clause'
   279	                          OR n.type LIKE '%_expression' OR n.type LIKE '%_arm'
   280	                          OR n.type LIKE '%_case' OR n.type LIKE '%_branch')
   281	                    THEN 1
   282	                END) AS conditionals,
   283	                -- Count loops: statements only, not keywords
   284	                -- (for_statement, while_statement, etc.)
   285	                COUNT(CASE
   286	                    WHEN is_loop(n.semantic_type)
   287	                     AND (n.type LIKE '%_statement' OR n.type LIKE '%_expression'
   288	                          OR n.type LIKE '%_loop')
   289	                    THEN 1
   290	                END) AS loops,
   291	                -- Max depth of any node in scope
   292	                MAX(n.depth) AS max_node_depth
   293	            FROM functions f
   294	            LEFT JOIN ast n
   295	              ON n.node_id > f.func_id
   296	             AND n.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   297	            WHERE
   298	                -- Exclude nodes inside nested functions
   299	                NOT EXISTS (
   300	                    SELECT 1 FROM nested_funcs nf
   301	                    WHERE nf.parent_func_id = f.func_id
   302	                      AND n.node_id > nf.nested_id
   303	                      AND n.node_id <= nf.nested_id + nf.nested_count
   304	                )
   305	            GROUP BY f.func_id, f.file_path, f.name, f.language,
   306	                     f.start_line, f.end_line, f.func_depth
   307	        )
   308	    SELECT
   309	        fm.file_path,
   310	        fm.name,
   311	        fm.language,
   312	        fm.start_line,
   313	        fm.end_line,
   314	        fm.end_line - fm.start_line + 1 AS lines,
   315	        fm.return_count,
   316	        fm.conditionals,
   317	        fm.loops,
   318	        fm.conditionals + fm.loops + 1 AS cyclomatic,
   319	        COALESCE(fm.max_node_depth::INTEGER - fm.func_depth::INTEGER, 0) AS max_depth
   320	    FROM function_metrics fm
   321	    ORDER BY fm.file_path, fm.start_line;
   322	
   323	-- Find functions that contain a specific node type
   324	-- Returns functions with at least one matching node in their scope.
   325	-- Uses function scope (excludes nested function internals).
   326	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_functions_containing('src/**/*.py', 'try_statement')
   327	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_functions_containing('src/**/*.py', 'call') WHERE match_name = 'eval'
   328	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_functions_containing(source, target_type, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   329	    WITH
   330	        ast AS (
   331	            SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   332	        ),
   333	        -- All function definitions
   334	        functions AS (
   335	            SELECT
   336	                a.node_id AS func_id,
   337	                a.name AS func_name,
   338	                a.file_path,
   339	                a.language,
   340	                a.start_line,
   341	                a.end_line,
   342	                a.descendant_count
   343	            FROM ast a
   344	            WHERE is_function_definition(a.semantic_type)
   345	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   346	        ),
   347	        -- For each function, identify nested functions within it
   348	        nested_funcs AS (
   349	            SELECT
   350	                nf.node_id AS nested_id,
   351	                nf.descendant_count AS nested_count,
   352	                f.func_id AS parent_func_id
   353	            FROM functions f
   354	            JOIN ast nf
   355	              ON nf.node_id > f.func_id
   356	             AND nf.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   357	             AND is_function_definition(nf.semantic_type)
   358	        ),
   359	        -- Find matching nodes in each function's scope
   360	        matches AS (
   361	            SELECT
   362	                f.func_id,
   363	                f.func_name,
   364	                f.file_path,
   365	                f.language,
   366	                f.start_line,
   367	                f.end_line,
   368	                n.node_id AS match_node_id,
   369	                n.name AS match_name,
   370	                n.type AS match_type,
   371	                n.start_line AS match_line,
   372	                n.peek AS match_peek
   373	            FROM functions f
   374	            JOIN ast n
   375	              ON n.node_id > f.func_id
   376	             AND n.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   377	             AND n.type = target_type
   378	            WHERE
   379	                -- Exclude nodes inside nested functions
   380	                NOT EXISTS (
   381	                    SELECT 1 FROM nested_funcs nf
   382	                    WHERE nf.parent_func_id = f.func_id
   383	                      AND n.node_id > nf.nested_id
   384	                      AND n.node_id <= nf.nested_id + nf.nested_count
   385	                )
   386	        )
   387	    SELECT
   388	        m.file_path,
   389	        m.func_name,
   390	        m.language,
   391	        m.start_line AS func_start_line,
   392	        m.end_line AS func_end_line,
   393	        m.match_name,
   394	        m.match_line,
   395	        m.match_peek
   396	    FROM matches m
   397	    ORDER BY m.file_path, func_start_line, m.match_line;
   398	
   399	-- Analyze nesting depth per function
   400	-- Returns depth statistics for identifying deeply nested code.
   401	-- Uses function scope (excludes nested function internals).
   402	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_nesting_analysis('src/**/*.py') WHERE max_depth > 5
   403	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_nesting_analysis(source, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   404	    WITH
   405	        ast AS (
   406	            SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   407	        ),
   408	        -- All function definitions
   409	        functions AS (
   410	            SELECT
   411	                a.node_id AS func_id,
   412	                a.name,
   413	                a.file_path,
   414	                a.language,
   415	                a.start_line,
   416	                a.end_line,
   417	                a.depth AS func_depth,
   418	                a.descendant_count
   419	            FROM ast a
   420	            WHERE is_function_definition(a.semantic_type)
   421	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   422	        ),
   423	        -- For each function, identify nested functions within it
   424	        nested_funcs AS (
   425	            SELECT
   426	                nf.node_id AS nested_id,
   427	                nf.descendant_count AS nested_count,
   428	                f.func_id AS parent_func_id
   429	            FROM functions f
   430	            JOIN ast nf
   431	              ON nf.node_id > f.func_id
   432	             AND nf.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   433	             AND is_function_definition(nf.semantic_type)
   434	        ),
   435	        -- Compute depth statistics for each function
   436	        depth_stats AS (
   437	            SELECT
   438	                f.func_id,
   439	                f.file_path,
   440	                f.name,
   441	                f.language,
   442	                f.start_line,
   443	                f.end_line,
   444	                f.func_depth,
   445	                -- Max relative depth (cast to INTEGER to avoid UINT32 overflow)
   446	                MAX(n.depth::INTEGER - f.func_depth::INTEGER) AS max_depth,
   447	                -- Average relative depth
   448	                ROUND(AVG(n.depth::INTEGER - f.func_depth::INTEGER), 2) AS avg_depth,
   449	                -- Count of deeply nested nodes (relative depth > 5)
   450	                COUNT(CASE WHEN n.depth::INTEGER - f.func_depth::INTEGER > 5 THEN 1 END) AS deep_nodes,
   451	                -- Total nodes in scope
   452	                COUNT(*) AS total_nodes
   453	            FROM functions f
   454	            LEFT JOIN ast n
   455	              ON n.node_id > f.func_id
   456	             AND n.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   457	            WHERE
   458	                -- Exclude nodes inside nested functions
   459	                NOT EXISTS (
   460	                    SELECT 1 FROM nested_funcs nf
   461	                    WHERE nf.parent_func_id = f.func_id
   462	                      AND n.node_id > nf.nested_id
   463	                      AND n.node_id <= nf.nested_id + nf.nested_count
   464	                )
   465	            GROUP BY f.func_id, f.file_path, f.name, f.language,
   466	                     f.start_line, f.end_line, f.func_depth
   467	        )
   468	    SELECT
   469	        ds.file_path,
   470	        ds.name,
   471	        ds.language,
   472	        ds.start_line,
   473	        ds.end_line,
   474	        ds.max_depth,
   475	        ds.avg_depth,
   476	        ds.deep_nodes,
   477	        ds.total_nodes
   478	    FROM depth_stats ds
   479	    ORDER BY ds.max_depth DESC, ds.file_path, ds.start_line;
   480	
   481	-- Automated security concern detection
   482	-- Scans for common security anti-patterns across languages.
   483	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_security_audit('src/**/*.py') WHERE risk_level = 'high'
   484	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_security_audit(source, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   485	    WITH
   486	        ast AS (
   487	            SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   488	        ),
   489	        -- Define security patterns to detect
   490	        security_patterns AS (
   491	            SELECT * FROM (VALUES
   492	                -- Code Injection (High Risk)
   493	                ('eval', 'Code Injection', 'high', 'Dynamic code evaluation'),
   494	                ('exec', 'Code Injection', 'high', 'Dynamic code execution'),
   495	                ('compile', 'Code Injection', 'high', 'Dynamic code compilation'),
   496	                ('Function', 'Code Injection', 'high', 'Dynamic function creation'),
   497	                ('setInterval', 'Code Injection', 'medium', 'Potential code injection via string'),
   498	                ('setTimeout', 'Code Injection', 'medium', 'Potential code injection via string'),
   499	                -- Command Injection (High Risk)
   500	                ('system', 'Command Injection', 'high', 'Shell command execution'),
   501	                ('popen', 'Command Injection', 'high', 'Shell command execution'),
   502	                ('spawn', 'Command Injection', 'high', 'Process spawning'),
   503	                ('execSync', 'Command Injection', 'high', 'Synchronous command execution'),
   504	                ('execFile', 'Command Injection', 'high', 'File execution'),
   505	                ('ShellExecute', 'Command Injection', 'high', 'Windows shell execution'),
   506	                -- Deserialization (High Risk)
   507	                ('pickle.load', 'Deserialization', 'high', 'Unsafe pickle deserialization'),
   508	                ('pickle.loads', 'Deserialization', 'high', 'Unsafe pickle deserialization'),
   509	                ('yaml.load', 'Deserialization', 'high', 'Unsafe YAML loading'),
   510	                ('unserialize', 'Deserialization', 'high', 'PHP unsafe deserialization'),
   511	                ('Marshal.load', 'Deserialization', 'high', 'Ruby unsafe deserialization'),
   512	                ('readObject', 'Deserialization', 'medium', 'Java deserialization'),
   513	                -- SQL Injection indicators (Medium Risk)
   514	                ('execute', 'SQL Injection', 'medium', 'SQL execution - verify parameterized'),
   515	                ('executemany', 'SQL Injection', 'medium', 'SQL execution - verify parameterized'),
   516	                ('raw', 'SQL Injection', 'medium', 'Raw SQL query'),
   517	                ('rawQuery', 'SQL Injection', 'medium', 'Raw SQL query'),
   518	                -- File Operations (Medium Risk)
   519	                ('readFile', 'Path Traversal', 'medium', 'File read - verify path sanitization'),
   520	                ('writeFile', 'Path Traversal', 'medium', 'File write - verify path sanitization'),
   521	                ('unlink', 'Path Traversal', 'medium', 'File deletion - verify path sanitization'),
   522	                ('rmdir', 'Path Traversal', 'medium', 'Directory deletion - verify path'),
   523	                -- Crypto Concerns (Medium Risk)
   524	                ('md5', 'Weak Crypto', 'medium', 'MD5 is cryptographically weak'),
   525	                ('sha1', 'Weak Crypto', 'medium', 'SHA1 is cryptographically weak'),
   526	                ('DES', 'Weak Crypto', 'medium', 'DES encryption is weak'),
   527	                ('RC4', 'Weak Crypto', 'medium', 'RC4 encryption is weak'),
   528	                -- Debug/Development (Low Risk)
   529	                ('console.log', 'Debug Code', 'low', 'Debug logging in code'),
   530	                ('print', 'Debug Code', 'low', 'Print statement - may leak info'),
   531	                ('debugger', 'Debug Code', 'low', 'Debugger statement'),
   532	                ('assert', 'Debug Code', 'low', 'Assert statement')
   533	            ) AS t(pattern_name, risk_category, risk_level, description)
   534	        ),
   535	        -- Find all function calls in the AST
   536	        calls AS (
   537	            SELECT
   538	                a.node_id,
   539	                a.file_path,
   540	                a.language,
   541	                a.name,
   542	                a.start_line,
   543	                a.peek
   544	            FROM ast a
   545	            WHERE is_function_call(a.semantic_type)
   546	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   547	        ),
   548	        -- Find the containing function for each call
   549	        containing_funcs AS (
   550	            SELECT
   551	                c.node_id AS call_node_id,
   552	                f.name AS function_name
   553	            FROM calls c
   554	            LEFT JOIN ast f
   555	              ON f.node_id < c.node_id
   556	             AND c.node_id <= f.node_id + f.descendant_count
   557	             AND is_function_definition(f.semantic_type)
   558	             AND f.name IS NOT NULL AND f.name != ''
   559	            QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY c.node_id ORDER BY f.node_id DESC) = 1
   560	        ),
   561	        -- Match calls against security patterns
   562	        -- Checks call name and peek (context) for qualified names like pickle.load
   563	        findings AS (
   564	            SELECT
   565	                c.file_path,
   566	                c.language,
   567	                c.start_line,
   568	                COALESCE(cf.function_name, '<module>') AS function_name,
   569	                p.risk_category,
   570	                p.risk_level,
   571	                p.description AS finding,
   572	                c.name AS matched_pattern,
   573	                c.peek AS context
   574	            FROM calls c
   575	            JOIN security_patterns p
   576	              ON c.name = p.pattern_name
   577	              OR c.name LIKE '%.' || p.pattern_name
   578	              OR c.name LIKE p.pattern_name || '.%'
   579	              OR c.peek LIKE '%' || p.pattern_name || '%'
   580	            LEFT JOIN containing_funcs cf ON cf.call_node_id = c.node_id
   581	        )
   582	    SELECT
   583	        fi.file_path,
   584	        fi.language,
   585	        fi.start_line,
   586	        fi.function_name,
   587	        fi.risk_category,
   588	        fi.risk_level,
   589	        fi.finding,
   590	        fi.matched_pattern,
   591	        fi.context
   592	    FROM findings fi
   593	    ORDER BY
   594	        CASE fi.risk_level WHEN 'high' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END,
   595	        fi.file_path, fi.start_line;
   596	
   597	-- Find potentially dead (unused) code
   598	-- Identifies functions and classes that are never referenced in the codebase.
   599	-- Note: This is heuristic - cannot detect dynamic usage or cross-file references
   600	-- when analyzing a single file. Best used on entire codebase.
   601	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_dead_code('src/**/*.py')
   602	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_dead_code(source, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   603	    WITH
   604	        ast AS (
   605	            SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   606	        ),
   607	        -- All function definitions
   608	        function_defs AS (
   609	            SELECT
   610	                a.node_id,
   611	                a.name,
   612	                a.file_path,
   613	                a.language,
   614	                a.start_line,
   615	                a.end_line,
   616	                a.type,
   617	                'function' AS definition_type
   618	            FROM ast a
   619	            WHERE is_function_definition(a.semantic_type)
   620	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   621	              -- Exclude special methods (constructors, dunder methods, etc.)
   622	              AND a.name NOT LIKE '\_\_%' ESCAPE '\'
   623	              AND a.name NOT IN ('main', 'setup', 'teardown', 'init', 'constructor')
   624	        ),
   625	        -- All class definitions
   626	        class_defs AS (
   627	            SELECT
   628	                a.node_id,
   629	                a.name,
   630	                a.file_path,
   631	                a.language,
   632	                a.start_line,
   633	                a.end_line,
   634	                a.type,
   635	                'class' AS definition_type
   636	            FROM ast a
   637	            WHERE is_class_definition(a.semantic_type)
   638	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   639	        ),
   640	        -- All definitions combined
   641	        all_defs AS (
   642	            SELECT * FROM function_defs
   643	            UNION ALL
   644	            SELECT * FROM class_defs
   645	        ),
   646	        -- All function calls (references to functions)
   647	        function_calls AS (
   648	            SELECT DISTINCT a.name
   649	            FROM ast a
   650	            WHERE is_function_call(a.semantic_type)
   651	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   652	        ),
   653	        -- All identifier references (for classes and other references)
   654	        -- Exclude identifiers that are the name of a definition (parent is definition type)
   655	        identifier_refs AS (
   656	            SELECT DISTINCT a.name
   657	            FROM ast a
   658	            LEFT JOIN ast parent ON parent.node_id = a.parent_id
   659	            WHERE is_identifier(a.semantic_type)
   660	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   661	              -- Exclude identifiers that are names within definitions
   662	              AND (parent.semantic_type IS NULL
   663	                   OR (NOT is_function_definition(parent.semantic_type)
   664	                       AND NOT is_class_definition(parent.semantic_type)))
   665	        ),
   666	        -- All references combined
   667	        all_refs AS (
   668	            SELECT name FROM function_calls
   669	            UNION
   670	            SELECT name FROM identifier_refs
   671	        ),
   672	        -- Find definitions that are never referenced
   673	        dead_code AS (
   674	            SELECT
   675	                d.file_path,
   676	                d.name,
   677	                d.language,
   678	                d.start_line,
   679	                d.end_line,
   680	                d.type,
   681	                d.definition_type,
   682	                'Never referenced in codebase' AS reason
   683	            FROM all_defs d
   684	            WHERE NOT EXISTS (
   685	                SELECT 1 FROM all_refs r
   686	                WHERE r.name = d.name
   687	            )
   688	        )
   689	    SELECT
   690	        dc.file_path,
   691	        dc.name,
   692	        dc.language,
   693	        dc.start_line,
   694	        dc.end_line,
   695	        dc.type,
   696	        dc.definition_type,
   697	        dc.reason
   698	    FROM dead_code dc
   699	    ORDER BY dc.file_path, dc.start_line;
   700	
"}

structuredContent.result:
1	-- Tree Navigation Macros
     2	-- Functions for navigating AST parent-child relationships
     3	-- These leverage the DFS pre-order node_id assignment for O(1) subtree queries
     4	
     5	-- =============================================================================
     6	-- Direct Relationship Helpers
     7	-- =============================================================================
     8	
     9	-- Get immediate children of a node
    10	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_children(my_ast_table, parent_node_id)
    11	-- Note: First argument is a table name (string), second is the parent node_id
    12	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_children(ast_table, parent_node_id) AS TABLE
    13	    SELECT * FROM query_table(ast_table) WHERE parent_id = parent_node_id;
    14	
    15	-- Get arguments of a function call node
    16	-- Returns the actual argument nodes (excludes punctuation like parentheses and commas)
    17	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_call_arguments(my_ast_table, call_node_id)
    18	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_call_arguments(ast_table, call_node_id) AS TABLE
    19	    WITH
    20	        -- Find the argument_list child of the call node
    21	        arg_list AS (
    22	            SELECT node_id as arg_list_id
    23	            FROM query_table(ast_table)
    24	            WHERE parent_id = call_node_id
    25	              AND type IN ('argument_list', 'arguments', 'actual_parameters')
    26	            LIMIT 1
    27	        ),
    28	        -- Get children of argument_list, excluding punctuation
    29	        args AS (
    30	            SELECT
    31	                ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY a.node_id) - 1 AS arg_position,
    32	                a.node_id AS arg_node_id,
    33	                a.name AS arg_name,
    34	                a.type AS arg_type,
    35	                a.peek AS arg_peek,
    36	                a.semantic_type,
    37	                a.start_line,
    38	                a.end_line
    39	            FROM query_table(ast_table) a, arg_list al
    40	            WHERE a.parent_id = al.arg_list_id
    41	              AND a.type NOT IN ('(', ')', ',', 'comment')
    42	        )
    43	    SELECT * FROM args;
    44	
    45	-- Get all definitions (functions, classes, variables, etc.) with unified categories
    46	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_definitions('src/**/*.py')
    47	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_definitions('src/main.py', language := 'python')
    48	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_definitions(source, language := NULL) AS TABLE
    49	    WITH ast AS (
    50	        SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
    51	    )
    52	    SELECT
    53	        a.name,
    54	        CASE
    55	            WHEN is_function_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'function'
    56	            WHEN is_class_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'class'
    57	            WHEN is_variable_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'variable'
    58	            WHEN is_module_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'module'
    59	            WHEN is_type_definition(a.semantic_type) THEN 'type'
    60	            ELSE 'other'
    61	        END AS definition_type,
    62	        a.language,
    63	        a.file_path,
    64	        a.start_line,
    65	        a.end_line,
    66	        a.node_id,
    67	        a.type,
    68	        a.semantic_type
    69	    FROM ast a
    70	    WHERE is_definition(a.semantic_type)
    71	      AND is_construct(a.flags)
    72	      AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
    73	    ORDER BY a.file_path, a.start_line;
    74	
    75	-- Get all descendants of a node (entire subtree)
    76	-- Uses descendant_count for O(1) range-based lookup (nodes are in DFS pre-order)
    77	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_descendants(my_ast_table, ancestor_node_id)
    78	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_descendants(ast_table, ancestor_node_id) AS TABLE
    79	    WITH ancestor AS (
    80	        SELECT node_id, descendant_count
    81	        FROM query_table(ast_table)
    82	        WHERE node_id = ancestor_node_id
    83	    )
    84	    SELECT a.*
    85	    FROM query_table(ast_table) a, ancestor anc
    86	    WHERE a.node_id > anc.node_id
    87	      AND a.node_id <= anc.node_id + anc.descendant_count;
    88	
    89	-- Get ancestors of a node (path from node to root)
    90	-- Uses recursive CTE following parent_id upward
    91	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_ancestors(my_ast_table, child_node_id)
    92	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_ancestors(ast_table, child_node_id) AS TABLE
    93	    WITH RECURSIVE ancestors AS (
    94	        SELECT * FROM query_table(ast_table) WHERE node_id = child_node_id
    95	        UNION ALL
    96	        SELECT a.*
    97	        FROM query_table(ast_table) a
    98	        JOIN ancestors anc ON a.node_id = anc.parent_id
    99	        WHERE anc.parent_id IS NOT NULL
   100	    )
   101	    SELECT * FROM ancestors;
   102	
   103	-- Get sibling nodes (same parent, excluding self)
   104	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_siblings(my_ast_table, target_node_id)
   105	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_siblings(ast_table, target_node_id) AS TABLE
   106	    WITH target AS (
   107	        SELECT parent_id FROM query_table(ast_table) WHERE node_id = target_node_id
   108	    )
   109	    SELECT a.*
   110	    FROM query_table(ast_table) a, target t
   111	    WHERE a.parent_id = t.parent_id
   112	      AND a.node_id != target_node_id;
   113	
   114	-- =============================================================================
   115	-- Line-Based Navigation
   116	-- =============================================================================
   117	
   118	-- Find all nodes that contain a specific line
   119	-- Returns nodes ordered by specificity (smallest span first)
   120	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_containing_line('src/main.py', 42)
   121	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_containing_line(source, line_num, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   122	    WITH ast AS (
   123	        SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   124	    )
   125	    SELECT a.*
   126	    FROM ast a
   127	    WHERE a.start_line <= line_num AND a.end_line >= line_num
   128	    ORDER BY (a.end_line - a.start_line), a.start_line;
   129	
   130	-- Get all nodes within a line range
   131	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_in_range('src/main.py', 10, 20)
   132	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_in_range(source, range_start, range_end, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   133	    WITH ast AS (
   134	        SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   135	    )
   136	    SELECT a.*
   137	    FROM ast a
   138	    WHERE a.start_line >= range_start AND a.end_line <= range_end;
   139	
   140	-- =============================================================================
   141	-- Scope-Aware Helpers
   142	-- =============================================================================
   143	
   144	-- Get all nodes inside a function, EXCLUDING nested function bodies
   145	-- This is essential for accurate complexity analysis - avoids double-counting
   146	-- nested function internals as part of the outer function's complexity.
   147	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_function_scope(my_ast_table, function_node_id)
   148	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_function_scope(ast_table, func_node_id) AS TABLE
   149	    WITH
   150	        -- Get the function node itself
   151	        func AS (
   152	            SELECT node_id, descendant_count
   153	            FROM query_table(ast_table)
   154	            WHERE node_id = func_node_id
   155	        ),
   156	        -- Get all descendants of this function
   157	        descendants AS (
   158	            SELECT a.*
   159	            FROM query_table(ast_table) a, func f
   160	            WHERE a.node_id > f.node_id
   161	              AND a.node_id <= f.node_id + f.descendant_count
   162	        ),
   163	        -- Find nested function definitions (excluding the function itself)
   164	        nested_funcs AS (
   165	            SELECT node_id, descendant_count
   166	            FROM descendants
   167	            WHERE is_function_definition(semantic_type)
   168	        )
   169	    -- Return descendants that are NOT inside any nested function
   170	    SELECT d.*
   171	    FROM descendants d
   172	    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
   173	        SELECT 1 FROM nested_funcs nf
   174	        WHERE d.node_id > nf.node_id
   175	          AND d.node_id <= nf.node_id + nf.descendant_count
   176	    );
   177	
   178	-- Get direct members of a class (methods, fields, nested classes)
   179	-- Returns the member definition nodes themselves, not their internal contents.
   180	-- Useful for class structure analysis without descending into method bodies.
   181	-- Usage: SELECT name, type FROM ast_class_members(my_ast_table, class_node_id)
   182	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_class_members(ast_table, class_node_id) AS TABLE
   183	    WITH
   184	        -- Get the class node
   185	        class AS (
   186	            SELECT node_id, descendant_count
   187	            FROM query_table(ast_table)
   188	            WHERE node_id = class_node_id
   189	        ),
   190	        -- Get all descendants of the class
   191	        descendants AS (
   192	            SELECT a.*
   193	            FROM query_table(ast_table) a, class c
   194	            WHERE a.node_id > c.node_id
   195	              AND a.node_id <= c.node_id + c.descendant_count
   196	        ),
   197	        -- Find all named definition nodes within the class
   198	        -- Excludes syntactic tokens like 'class'/'def' keywords via flag check
   199	        all_defs AS (
   200	            SELECT node_id, descendant_count, name, type, semantic_type,
   201	                   start_line, end_line, depth, parent_id, peek, file_path, language
   202	            FROM descendants
   203	            WHERE is_definition(semantic_type)
   204	              AND is_construct(flags)
   205	              AND name IS NOT NULL AND name != ''
   206	        )
   207	    -- Return definitions that are NOT nested inside other definitions
   208	    -- (i.e., direct members of the class, not locals inside methods)
   209	    SELECT d.*
   210	    FROM all_defs d
   211	    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
   212	        SELECT 1 FROM all_defs other
   213	        WHERE other.node_id != d.node_id
   214	          AND d.node_id > other.node_id
   215	          AND d.node_id <= other.node_id + other.descendant_count
   216	    );
   217	
   218	-- =============================================================================
   219	-- Analysis Macros
   220	-- =============================================================================
   221	
   222	-- Compute metrics for all functions in the AST
   223	-- Returns one row per function with complexity metrics.
   224	-- Uses function scope (excludes nested function internals) for accurate counts.
   225	-- Usage: SELECT * FROM ast_function_metrics('src/**/*.py') WHERE cyclomatic > 10
   226	CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO ast_function_metrics(source, language := NULL) AS TABLE
   227	    WITH
   228	        ast AS (
   229	            SELECT * FROM read_ast(source, language)
   230	        ),
   231	        -- All function definitions
   232	        functions AS (
   233	            SELECT
   234	                a.node_id AS func_id,
   235	                a.name,
   236	                a.file_path,
   237	                a.language,
   238	                a.start_line,
   239	                a.end_line,
   240	                a.depth AS func_depth,
   241	                a.descendant_count
   242	            FROM ast a
   243	            WHERE is_function_definition(a.semantic_type)
   244	              AND a.name IS NOT NULL AND a.name != ''
   245	        ),
   246	        -- For each function, identify nested functions within it
   247	        nested_funcs AS (
   248	            SELECT
   249	                nf.node_id AS nested_id,
   250	                nf.descendant_count AS nested_count,
   251	                f.func_id AS parent_func_id
   252	            FROM functions f
   253	            JOIN ast nf
   254	              ON nf.node_id > f.func_id
   255	             AND nf.node_id <= f.func_id + f.descendant_count
   256	             AND is_function_definition(nf.semantic_type)
   257	        ),
   258	        -- Compute metrics for each function
   259	        -- Counts only nodes in function scope (excludes nested function internals)
   260	        function_metrics AS (
   261	            SELECT
   262	                f.func_id,
   263	                f.file_path,
   264	                f.name,
   265	                f.language,
